data.title: Apple risk-reward 'skews positive' when multiple drops below 25: BofA
data.description:
The risk-reward on Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) skews positively three to six months after its earnings multiple has dropped below 25, Bank of America said.

“In recent weeks [Apple] stock has moved significantly to the downside (down 25% YTD and 23.5% since last earnings relative to SPX decline of 13.7% and 16.6% respectively),” analyst Wamsi Mohan wrote in a note to clients, referencing geopolitical uncertainties around tariffs and delays to a more personalized Siri. “In our view, the pullback represents a particularly buying opportunity for investors to own a high-quality name.” Mohan reiterated his Buy rating and $250 price target on the stock.

In the three months after Apple's P/E multiple has fallen below, the share price returned 7%, 8%, 14%, and 17% over the three, six, nine and 12 months after, respectively, Mohan added. Downside in the following three months ranged from a 5% to 11% decline in April and August 2022, but upside ranged from a 11% to 26% increase. 

“Given the geopolitical upheaval with tariffs and risk of potential escalation with China, there is a chance that the risk to estimates could be more severe than reflected in consensus,” Mohan added. However, he explained Apple has a number of options to mitigate downside risk to estimates, including getting more iPhones from India; raising pricing on products and services; pressuring its supply chain for better pricing; ad introducing new products at higher price points and changing the cadence of releases.

Apple shares rose 3.4% in early trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Brera receives FIGC approval for acquisition of Juve Stabia stake
data.description:
• Brera Holdings (NASDAQ:BREA) Tuesday said that the Italian Football Federation has officially completed its review and approved the company’s acquisition of a stake in Juve Stabia, a professional football club currently competing in Italy’s Serie B.
  • Following a comprehensive regulatory process conducted by the FIGC’s Commissione Acquisizioni Partecipazioni Societarie, the commission confirmed that Brera Holdings satisfies the standards of financial soundness and reputational integrity required by FIGC’s regulations.
  • Brera Holdings (NASDAQ:BREA) shares up 7.7% to $0.70.

data.title: Santander evaluating options for $8B Polish stake - Bloomberg
data.description:
Banco Satander (NYSE:SAN) is considering options for its majority stake in its Polish business, including a possible sale. Santander ADRs rose 6.1%. 

The Spanish bank is using an adviser to evaluate its 62% stake in Santander Bank Polska SA and is reaching out to potential investors to see about their interest, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Santander's stake in the unit is valued at about $8 billion.

Deliberations are preliminary and Santander (NYSE:SAN) may decide to retain the Polish business or only pursue a partial sale of its stake, according to the report.

Santander declined to comment to Bloomberg.

data.title: Outlook Therapeutics gets FDA review acceptance for wet AMD treatment
data.description:
• Outlook Therapeutics (NASDAQ:OTLK) said on Tuesday that the U.S. FDA has accepted to review ONS-5010, an ophthalmic formulation of bevacizumab, for the treatment of wet age-related macular degeneration.
  • Stock is up 9% in morning trade.
  • The agency has acknowledged receipt of the resubmission of the Biologics License Application for ONS-5010, after the company reported in January that ONS-5010 demonstrated non-inferiority to ranibizumab, sold by Roche's (OTCQX:RHHBY) Genentech unit, at week 12 in a study. 
  • FDA set a Prescription Drug User Fee Act goal date of August 27, 2025.
  • The company  said that ONS-5010, if approved, will be branded as Lytenava in the United States for the treatment of wet AMD and is expected to receive 12 years of regulatory exclusivity.
  • ONS-5010 has already received market authorization in the European Union and United Kingdom.

data.title: Eni to spend €24B in North Africa over next four years, CEO says
data.description:
Eni (NYSE:E) - already a major foreign investor in North Africa's energy sector - expects to spend ~€24B ($26.2B) in Algeria, Libya and Egypt over the next four years to help boost energy production, CEO Claudio Descalzi said Tuesday, according to Reuters.

The CEO said the three countries can play an important role as hydrocarbon suppliers for Europe, but need outside investment to expand their energy production and meet rising domestic demand.

"Internal demand in these countries, because of demographic growth, is increasing at about 7%-8% every year... this means they need gas, they need investment," Descalzi told an energy conference in Ravenna.

The investments would coincide with the Italian government's efforts to relaunch its economic and political ties with Africa as part of its so-called Mattei Plan.

Eni (NYSE:E) will continue to produce gas in Venezuela while engaging with the Trump administration over a waiver to export oil from the country, Descalzi also said.

Eni (E) has been producing gas in Venezuela for the domestic market since 2022, but it is paid in Venezuelan oil, which it exports; the U.S. told Eni last week that it would no longer be able to receive payments for the oil as part of efforts to place secondary sanctions on the country.

data.title: Rocket Companies upgraded to Equalweight at Barclays
data.description:
Barclays upgraded Rocket Companies (NYSE:RKT) to Equalweight from Underweight on the potential upside from its pending acquisition of Mr. Cooper (NASDAQ:COOP).

The combination of Mr. Cooper's (NASDAQ:COOP) market-leading servicing business with Rocket's (NYSE:RKT) strength in originations is transformative for Rocket, analyst Terry Ma said.

"The deal should create a more balanced business model at scale, and we estimate 24% EPS accretion in 2026," Ma wrote in a note to clients. "There are dis-synergies (i.e., UWMC pulled subservice from COOP) and integration risks that need to be accounted for, but we think the long-term positives outweigh the near-term negatives."

The Equalweight rating on Rocket (RKT) aligns with the SA Quant rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Hold, and contrasts with the average SA Analyst rating of Buy.

Still, Rocket Cos. (RKT) stock slumped 5.5% in Tuesday morning trading.

data.title: J&J upgraded, Pfizer, AbbVie downgraded as Goldman reviews U.S. biopharma coverage
data.description:
Goldman Sachs made significant rating changes across its U.S. biopharma coverage on Tuesday, upgrading the likes of Eli Lilly (LLY) and Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) while downgrading Bristol Myers (NYSE:BMY), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), and AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV).

Eli Lilly (LLY) has ended a three-day selloff after Goldman Sachs analyst Asad Haider upgraded the Indiana-based drugmaker to Buy from Neutral, citing an attractive entry point given its leadership in the obesity drug market. However, the firm lowered its price target on LLY to $888 from $892 per share.

Upgrading J&J (NYSE:JNJ) to Buy from Neutral, Haider argued that concerns surrounding the loss of exclusivity (LOE) for the company’s blockbuster drug Stelara were overdone. A historical pattern showing that pharma multiples usually trough roughly a year ahead of major LOEs “could bode well for JNJ if this pattern is followed,” he wrote. Goldman issued a 12-month per-share price target of $172 on JNJ, up from its previous target of $157.  

However, Haider downgraded AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) to Neutral from Buy, and trimmed its 12-month price target to $194 from $212 per share, arguing that in the wake of Humira LOE, Wall Street has fully appreciated the growth prospects for the company’s new immunology drugs Skyrizi and Rinvoq. “With no major pipeline catalysts ahead, this puts the onus for positive surprises to emerge out of other parts of ABBV’s portfolio where we see a tougher near-term setup,” Haider wrote.

Goldman downgraded Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) to Neutral from Buy and lowered its 12-month price target to $25 from $32. Haider noted that the company's recent strategic efforts, such as cost-cutting and redeployment of COVID cash flow towards oncology-related M&A recently, can take time to get reflected in its stock price. “The fruits of these efforts are a multi-year journey that will take time to get recognized and reflected in the stock price, in our view,” he added.

Haider downgraded Bristol Myers (NYSE:BMY) to Neutral from Buy and trimmed its 12-month price target to $55 from $67 per share, arguing that the company’s recent moves, such as cost containment efforts designed to face upcoming patent cliffs, are adequately reflected in its stock price.

“While we believe the magnitude of BMY’s “stacked” LOE exposure is well understood and reflected in valuation, there remains a significant gap to bridge the expected declines in revenues in the coming years,” the analyst wrote.

data.title: Goldman Sachs cuts outlook for airline industry, drops American to sell
data.description:
With its macro team now forecasting a 45% probability of a U.S. recession, and taking into account increasing economic and geopolitical uncertainty, Goldman Sachs makes a “material” cut to the level of demand for air travel in 2025 and lowered its outlook for the U.S. airline industry.

While airlines should perform better than in prior slowdowns thanks to offsetting capacity cuts, consolidation within the industry, and more diversified revenue, Goldman lowered its target multiples across its airline coverage and consequently, downgraded American Airlines to Sell from Neutral.

“American Airlines’ relatively higher balance sheet leverage and operating leverage drive significantly larger cuts to our outlook versus the industry…and as profitability across the industry and at American is negatively impact by lower demand, we are now forecasting lower free cash flow at American,” says Goldman Sachs analyst Catherine O’Brien.

Given O’Brien’s reduced outlook for American (NASDAQ:AAL), she now expects 2025 free cash flow to be more than cut in half, which drives net debt-to-EBITDAR higher year-over-year to 5.0x from 4.2x, previously. O’Brien also slashes American’s (NASDAQ:AAL) price target in half to $8 and sets EPS estimates below the street’s consensus estimates. American’s (NASDAQ:AAL) higher operating leverage will have a more pronounced impact to revenue, and ultimately EPS with O’Brien forecasting 2025/2026 EPS at $0.90/$2.45 versus the Street’s consensus of $1.74/$2.62.

At the same time, O’Brien upgraded SkyWest (NASDAQ:SKYW) to Buy from Neutral, seen as one of the most defensive names in Goldman’s coverage given the company’s largely contractual revenue structure, strong balance sheet, and solid free cash flow profile.

“We expect SkyWest’s revenue and earnings to be significantly less impacted by the current economic backdrop than the rest of our U.S. airlines coverage,” O’Brien says, calling out SkyWest’s purchase agreements with its partner airlines. Last year, SkyWest (NASDAQ:SKYW) generated 84% of its revenue with its partners due to the loss of pilots post-pandemic. Under these capacity purchase agreements, SkyWest (NASDAQ:SKYW) is paid a fixed rate primarily based on operational metrics, making the carrier significantly less exposed to the fare environment that other carriers.

To reflect the increased geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty across the industry, O’Brien lowered her price targets for Buy-rated Alaska Air Group (ALK) by 16% to $71, Neutral-rated Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) by 39% to $56, Sell-rated JetBlue (JBLU) 45% to $3, Sell-rated Southwest Airlines (LUV) by 18% to $23, Neutral-rated Sun Country (SNCY) 23% to $13, and Buy-rated United (UAL) by 30% to $92. Buy-rated Delta (DAL) remains unchanged at $60.

data.title: The Fed unlike to cut until it sees clear struggling signs in the labor market – TS Lombard
data.description:
TS Lombard economists believe the Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut interest rates until it is clear the labor market is struggling.

Odds for an interest rate cut on May 7 are down to 26.5%, according to the CME FedWatch.

“Textbook central banking would counsel looking through a negative supply shock such as tariffs, and we have long maintained that inflation over 2% and up to 3% will be implicitly countenanced by the Fed,” analysts said.

Also, “with its dual mandate, the bank did not want to create a recession in order to squeeze out the last few percentage points of inflation above 2%,” they said.

But the current dynamics, such as the markets performance and expectations for rising inflation “flip the focus back to the price stability side of the Fed’s mandate and makes market pricing of five cuts in 2025 seem overly optimistic.”

Rate cut forecasts by market analysts vary. BlackRock's Larry Fink said he sees “zero chance” of a rate cut in 2025. Morgan Stanley also sees no rate cuts this year. Goldman Sachs sees three consecutive 25 basis point rate cuts beginning in June.

data.title: Tobacco smoke signals: Investors may benefit from tariff environment while consumers lose out
data.description:
The tobacco sector is impacted by tariffs due to the higher costs for imported tobacco products such as cigars and other products. President Trump's recent tariffs imposed a 10% baseline rate on imports from countries like the Dominican Republic and Honduras, while Nicaragua faces an 18% tariff. 

In general, higher tariff-related costs have been successfully passed on to consumers through price increases for products like cigars and accessories, which has left the tobacco giants relatively unscathed. Reports indicate that may be the case with the most recent tariff actions by the U.S., as higher prices on tobacco products in the U.S. will reportedly be seen within weeks.

Looking further ahead, analysts have warned that companies importing raw tobacco or finished products that face increased operational tariff-related expenses could reach a limit in passing on the extra costs to consumers and see demand trickle lower. 

Over the last six weeks, Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) scratched out a 0.3% gain and Imperial Brands (OTCQX:IMBBY) is 1.8% higher, while Altria (NYSE:MO) is down 2.8% and British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) is 1.1% lower. All four tobacco stocks have outperformed the broad market as investors have sought out defensive consumer staples favorites.
